narrative:
The market sits comfortably in a business as usual regime, with a stress score of 11 out of 100 confirming an exceptionally calm environment. Broad conditions remain orderly across asset classes, with no meaningful divergences pulling the reading toward any elevated quadrant.

At 15.8, the VIX sits at the 26th percentile of its ten-year range, a level that signals neither complacency nor concern, and the contango term structure confirms that options markets are not pricing in near-term disruption. Credit reinforces this picture emphatically: high yield spreads at 271 basis points sit at just the 7th percentile of their five-year history, with an OAS z-score of -1.13, and investment grade at 75 basis points tells a near-identical story at -1.14. Credit is not merely confirming the equity calm — it is running ahead of it, with spreads pricing in conditions tighter than roughly 93 percent of the recent historical distribution.

Sector breadth is unremarkable with only three of eleven sectors in the red, but the rotation within that narrow dispersion is worth noting. Defensive areas are taking the bulk of the pressure, with utilities down 3.2 percent over five days, real estate off 2.7 percent, and consumer staples falling 2.2 percent, while technology surged 7.1 percent and emerging market equities, led by EEM up 3.5 percent and FXI up 1.7 percent, showed genuine strength. The pattern is consistent with a risk-on tilt inside an otherwise stable tape, with capital rotating away from rate-sensitive defensives and toward growth and international exposure.
